title: String Tools
description: Find the length of a string and access individual characters.

slides.title: String Length 📏
slides.content:
To get the length of a string, use the `length()` function.

Since a string is technically an object in C++, it has built-in 'functions' you can call by using a dot `.`.

slides.title: Length Example
slides.content: Getting the length of the alphabet.
slides.codeSnippet:
string txt = "ABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ";
cout << "The length is: " << txt.length();

quizData.question: What is the output of the length() function above?
quizData.explanation: The length() function counts the number of characters in the string, which is 26 letters.

slides.title: size() function 🛠️
slides.content:
You might see some C++ programs use the `size()` function to get the length of a string instead of `length()`.
Both do exactly the same thing. It's just an alias (`txt.size()`).

slides.title: Strings as Arrays? 🧐
slides.content:
Did you know that a string is simply an array of characters? 
That means you can access the characters in a string by referring to its index number inside square brackets `[]`, exactly like an array!

quizData.question: If string word = "Hello"; what does cout << word[0]; print?
quizData.explanation: Because strings are arrays of characters, the character at the start (index 0) is 'H'.
